# Design Partner Pilot Blueprint and Rollout Runbook

## Summary
Defines the repeatable pilot model, qualification gates, rollout phases, and scorecards for proving digital employees with real customers.

## Extracted Preview
> RUH.AI
> V1 Design Partner Pilot Blueprint & Rollout Runbook
> A repeatable deployment playbook for proving digital employees with real customers, using packs, tenant overlays, approvals, and measured phase gates.
> Applies to: Construction Project Operations + AI BuildOps reference packs Audience: Product, engineering, implementation, customer success, design partners Status: Working v1 execution document
> What this document answers
> • Which customers qualify for a design-partner pilot and what must be true before kickoff.
> • How a pilot moves from monitor mode to draft mode, approval mode, and constrained autonomy.
> • Which metrics, roles, artifacts, and gate reviews determine pilot success and production expansion.
> 
> 1. Purpose of this document
> The previous documents define the platform. This one defines the path to prove it with real customers.
> 
> This blueprint converts the Ruh.ai product stack into an executable customer deployment model. It assumes the platform PRD, system design, API/event contracts, employee pack specification, reference pack definitions, and tenant overlay kits already exist. The pilot blueprint is the missing execution layer between product design and production rollout.
> Its job is simple: make sure every design partner is qualified consistently, deployed safely, measured against a baseline, and either promoted to production or exited with clean learnings that improve the packs, overlays, and platform.
> Qualify
> Confirm sponsor, workflow owner, systems of record, baseline metrics, and the trust boundary before work begins.
> Deploy
